source: Hengl, T. (2018). Global Landform and Lithology / USGS EcoTapestry

classes.name: Breaks / Foothills
classes.description: Transitional terrain between plains and uplands, with irregular slopes and dissected surfaces typical of piedmont zones.

classes.name: Flat Plains
classes.description: Nearly level lowlands with very gentle gradients, such as river floodplains, deltaic plains, and low-lying coastal areas.

classes.name: High Mountains / Deep Canyons
classes.description: Highest-relief terrain featuring rugged peaks, steep ridges, and deeply incised gorges, as in the Himalayas, Andes, or Rocky Mountains.

classes.name: Hills
classes.description: Moderately rolling terrain with rounded summits and gentle to moderate slopes, common across much of temperate Europe and eastern North America.

classes.name: Low Hills
classes.description: Gently undulating landscape with low relief and subdued slopes, often seen on eroded ancient plateaus or at the margins of plains.

classes.name: Low Mountains
classes.description: Upland terrain with distinct ridges and valleys but modest elevation, such as the Appalachians, Vosges, or Ural Mountains.

classes.name: Smooth Plains
classes.description: Extensive, nearly featureless flatlands with very low relief, typical of interior continental basins, glacial outwash plains, and alluvial fans.
